niche: WorthScan used futon and dorm furniture resale valuation

source_inputs.label: Operator brief
source_inputs.value: Create a WorthScan post for a used futon listing in student resale.

source_inputs.label: Valuation method
source_inputs.value: Check size, frame type, cushion condition, stains, odor risk, stairs/pickup hassle, and three local dorm furniture comps.

source_inputs.label: Research boundary
source_inputs.value: Use manual reviewed trend examples only; no scraping, account automation, or auto-posting.

trend_examples.hook: Free furniture can still cost you
trend_examples.notes: Pickup and condition risk can carry the post.

trend_examples.hook: Would you drag this futon up three flights?
trend_examples.notes: Specific hassle makes the valuation relatable.
Visible condition risk creates a clear before/after answer.

notes: No external calls or browser workflows.
Manual posting and manual metrics only.

slides.on_screen_text: Scan this futon before you rent a truck
slides.visual_direction: Show the futon, asking price, and pickup constraints.

slides.on_screen_text: Check size, frame, stains, and cushion wear
slides.visual_direction: Use detail crops for frame, cushion, and visible condition.
Show comp cards with size, condition, pickup, and price.

slides.on_screen_text: Subtract odor risk, stairs, and transport
Worth range: take it, negotiate, or skip
slides.visual_direction: Show final range, confidence, and comment CTA.

output_requirements.caption: Would you pick up this futon? Comment "scan" with the next dorm deal. WorthScan is an estimate, not a guarantee.
output_requirements.spoken_script: A futon price is not just the asking price. Check size, frame, cushion wear, stains, pickup difficulty, and transport cost, compare three nearby listings, then decide whether to take it, negotiate, or skip.
posting_notes: Avoid health or cleanliness claims beyond visible facts.
Do not include addresses or seller contact details.
